title: dactylorhiza majalis var. pumila
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis var. pumila, also known as the dwarf marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that is native to certain regions in Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza majalis var. pumila are attributed to its ability to stimulate blood flow and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant can be dried and processed into a powder, which can be used in teas, tinctures, or capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
